channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: On a flight home, a stranger helped her understand what came next
item.description: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.content:encoded: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kwerel

item.title: Volunteer tinkerers bring broken appliances back to life for people in need
item.description: Sometimes a broken appliance gets thrown out even though it just needs a little fix. That's where volunteer tinkerers come in. They make it work again and give it to people in need.
item.content:encoded: Sometimes a broken appliance gets thrown out even though it just needs a little fix. That's where volunteer tinkerers come in. They make it work again and give it to people in need.
item.dc:creator: Tana Weingartner

item.title: Shipping exec says industry needs assurances Strait of Hormuz will be safe
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Jakob Larsen, chief safety and security officer at BIMCO, the global shipping association, about what it might mean to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Jakob Larsen, chief safety and security officer at BIMCO, the global shipping association, about what it might mean to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Harland-Dunaway

item.title: Protests continue against Albania's government over a Kushner-linked resort project
item.description: Plans to develop a luxury resort that has links to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump have prompted a growing protest movement against Albania's government.
item.content:encoded: Plans to develop a luxury resort that has links to Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump have prompted a growing protest movement against Albania's government.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Jazz legend Abdullah Ibrahim, the man behind an anti-apartheid anthem, has died at 91
item.description: Jazz legend and anti-apartheid icon Abdullah Ibrahim has died at the age of 91. He leaves behind a global contribution to jazz music.
item.content:encoded: Jazz legend and anti-apartheid icon Abdullah Ibrahim has died at the age of 91. He leaves behind a global contribution to jazz music.
item.dc:creator: Marc Rivers

item.title: How to map quadrillions of miles of underground fungi
item.description: NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with ecologist Justin Stewart about mapping the complex network of fungi connecting the Earth's plants.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Florido speaks with ecologist Justin Stewart about mapping the complex network of fungi connecting the Earth's plants.
item.dc:creator: Zephyr Weinreich

item.title: From the middle of the ocean, Kelsey Pfendler on rowing solo across the pacific
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Kelsey Pfendler, who is rowing solo from California to Hawaii. She is attempting to become the first American woman to do so.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Kelsey Pfendler, who is rowing solo from California to Hawaii. She is attempting to become the first American woman to do so.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur

item.title: Trump's Iran agreement takes center stage at G7 summit
item.description: President Trump said the next stage of negotiations with Iran should be easier than the first as he continues to tout the recent agreement between the two countries at the G7 summit in France.
item.content:encoded: President Trump said the next stage of negotiations with Iran should be easier than the first as he continues to tout the recent agreement between the two countries at the G7 summit in France.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Utah mountain bike girls take no shortcuts
item.description: For some high school mountain bike races, varsity girls race shorter distances than boys. One 16-year-old athlete in Utah is trying to change that.
item.content:encoded: For some high school mountain bike races, varsity girls race shorter distances than boys. One 16-year-old athlete in Utah is trying to change that.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Hulet

item.title: Here's what's top of mind for Colorado voters in an increasingly rare swing district
item.description: In an era of partisan gerrymandering, Colorado's 8th Congressional District remains a toss-up. Voters, many unaffiliated, have a variety of opinions on what type of candidate they want this November.
item.content:encoded: In an era of partisan gerrymandering, Colorado's 8th Congressional District remains a toss-up. Voters, many unaffiliated, have a variety of opinions on what type of candidate they want this November.
item.dc:creator: Rae Solomon
